===============================================

Today is February 19, 2026. President Donald Trump recently spoke at the inaugural meeting of his Board of Peace. According to the reports, what was the original intended purpose of this board when it was first announced?
(A) To help end the war between Israel and Hamas in Gaza and oversee reconstruction
(B) To negotiate a new nuclear agreement with the Islamic Republic of Iran
(C) To replace the United Nations as the primary body for international conflict resolution
(D) To coordinate the surge of military forces and aircraft carriers to the Middle East
(E) To manage the diplomatic relations between the US, Israel, and Saudi Arabia
explanation: The article states that when Trump first announced the Board of Peace, "it was thought to be aimed at helping end the two-year war between Israel and Hamas in Gaza and oversee reconstruction."
model_output:
The original intended purpose of the Board of Peace, when first announced, was:

(A) To help end the war between Israel and Hamas in Gaza and oversee reconstruction

Reasoning:

- The Board of Peace was initially unveiled in September 2025 by President Trump as part of a **20-point plan to end the war in Gaza**, which included immediately ending hostilities with Hamas, facilitating the return of hostages, establishing transitional governance, and rebuilding Gaza. ([foreignpolicy.com](https://foreignpolicy.com/2025/10/06/israel-hamas-ceasefire-talks-trump-plan-egypt/?utm_source=openai))  
- It was specifically intended to oversee **Gaza peace plan implementation**, including reconstruction, demilitarization of Hamas, and governing support during transition. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Board_of_Peace?utm_source=openai))  
- Other options like negotiating a nuclear agreement with Iran (B), replacing the UN entirely as an international body (C), coordinating military surges to the Middle East (D), or managing relations between US/Israel/Saudi Arabia (E) do not align with what the board was initially announced for. While there are concerns that it could challenge or compete with the UN in broader terms, that was not the original *primary* purpose. ([aljazeera.com](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/1/18/trumps-board-of-peace-appears-to-seek-wider-mandate-beyond-gaza?utm_source=openai))  

<final_answer>  
(A)  
</final_answer>

(A) 15 rescuers

(C) Around 50 rescuers
(D) Exactly 11 rescuers
(E) Over 100 rescuers

model_output:

<final_answer>  
(E) Over 100 rescuers  
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is February 19, 2026. Official figures show the US trade deficit hit a new high last year. What was the specific deficit amount for goods and services combined?
(A) $1.2 trillion
(B) $3.4 trillion
(C) $202.1 billion
(D) $890 billion
(E) $901.5 billion
explanation: The article notes: "Overall, the deficit in goods and services - which takes into account things like travel and digital services - was $901.5bn last year."
model_output:
The correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Here’s how I arrived at that:

- According to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA), in 2025 the goods and services trade deficit was **$901.5 billion**. ([bea.gov](https://www.bea.gov/news/2026/us-international-trade-goods-and-services-december-and-annual-2025?utm_source=openai))  
- For comparison, exports were about $3,432.3 billion and imports about $4,333.8 billion that year, giving a net deficit of roughly $901.5 billion. ([bea.gov](https://www.bea.gov/news/2026/us-international-trade-goods-and-services-december-and-annual-2025?utm_source=openai))  

So among the options given, (E) $901.5 billion is the right figure.

Today is February 19, 2026. Under pressure from the US, acting Venezuelan leader Delcy Rodríguez has agreed to a significant oil deal. How many barrels of oil did Trump announce Caracas had agreed to deliver?
(A) 500 million barrels
(B) 50 million barrels
(C) 7.9 million barrels
(D) 6.5 million barrels
(E) 100 million barrels
explanation: The article states: "Trump announced that Caracas had agreed to deliver up to 50 million barrels of oil - and that he would manage the money."
model_output:
Based on the recent reports, Trump announced that Caracas had agreed to deliver **between 30 million and 50 million barrels** of oil. ([en.as.com](https://en.as.com/latest_news/trump-announces-that-venezuela-will-deliver-between-30-and-50-million-barrels-of-oil-f202601-n/?utm_source=openai))

Among the options given:

(A) 500 million barrels — too high  
(B) 50 million barrels — within the announced range  
(C) 7.9 million barrels — significantly lower  
(D) 6.5 million barrels — much lower  
(E) 100 million barrels — too high  

The correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Forbes: *“Venezuela will turn over 30 million to 50 million barrels of oil to the U.S.”* ([forbes.com](https://www.forbes.com/sites/antoniopequenoiv/2026/01/06/trump-says-venezuela-will-provide-us-with-30-million-to-50-million-oil-barrels-following-maduro-capture//?utm_source=openai))  
- AS USA: *“between 30 and 50 MILLION barrels of high-quality, sanctioned oil to the United States of America.”* ([en.as.com](https://en.as.com/latest_news/trump-announces-that-venezuela-will-deliver-between-30-and-50-million-barrels-of-oil-f202601-n/?utm_source=openai))

===============================================

Today is February 19, 2026. Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei recently posted an AI-generated image on social media. What did this image depict?
(A) A US aircraft carrier striking Tehran
(B) The USS Abraham Lincoln in the Strait of Hormuz
(C) The USS Gerald R Ford at the bottom of the ocean
(D) A nuclear explosion at an Iranian facility
(E) A meeting between US and Iranian negotiators in Switzerland
explanation: The article states: "Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei posted on X on Tuesday an AI-generated image of the Ford at the bottom of the ocean." (Referring to the USS Gerald R Ford).
model_output:
The correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

**Explanation:**

In mid-February 2026, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei posted an AI-generated image showing the **USS Gerald R. Ford aircraft carrier** heavily damaged and **sinking to the bottom of the sea**. ([indiatoday.in](https://www.indiatoday.in/amp/world/story/iran-supreme-leader-warns-us-warships-can-be-sunk-amid-nuclear-talks-glbs-2870509-2026-02-18?utm_source=openai))

Here’s how it matches up:

- **(A)** A US aircraft carrier striking Tehran — No evidence of that.
- **(B)** The USS Abraham Lincoln in the Strait of Hormuz — There was propaganda video depicting attacks on *that* carrier, but the image in question referred specifically to the *Gerald R. Ford*. ([indiatoday.in](https://www.indiatoday.in/amp/world/story/iran-supreme-leader-warns-us-warships-can-be-sunk-amid-nuclear-talks-glbs-2870509-2026-02-18?utm_source=openai))
- **(C)** The USS Gerald R Ford at the bottom of the ocean — This matches exactly what the AI image depicts. ([indiatoday.in](https://www.indiatoday.in/amp/world/story/iran-supreme-leader-warns-us-warships-can-be-sunk-amid-nuclear-talks-glbs-2870509-2026-02-18?utm_source=openai))
- **(D)** A nuclear explosion at an Iranian facility — No, that was not the content.
- **(E)** A meeting between US and Iranian negotiators in Switzerland — Also not what was shown.

So the right choice is (C).

===============================================

Today is February 19, 2026. Jesse Jackson was famous for his powerful oratory. Which specific refrain from his 1988 Democratic National Convention speech was later echoed by Barack Obama's 2008 campaign?
(A) Hope and change
(B) Rainbow coalition
(C) Yankee go home
(D) I have a dream
(E) Keep hope alive
explanation: The article states: "His speech to the 1988 Democratic National Convention, which ended with the refrain 'keep hope alive', would be echoed decades later in the 'hope and change' slogan of Obama's successful 2008 presidential campaign."
model_output:
The correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

**Reasoning:**

- In his 1988 Democratic National Convention speech, Jesse Jackson repeatedly ended with the refrain: “Keep hope alive. Keep hope alive. Keep hope alive. On tomorrow night and beyond, keep hope alive.” ([kwqc.com](https://www.kwqc.com/2026/02/17/jackson-told-america-keep-hope-alive-more-key-quotes-civil-rights-icon/?utm_source=openai))  
- Barack Obama’s 2008 campaign prominently used “Hope” and “Change” as its core message. The themes of hope and the slogan “Change we can believe in” were central to inspiring his supporters. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barack_Obama_2008_presidential_campaign?utm_source=openai))  
- Thus, the refrain from Jackson’s speech that was later echoed by Obama is *“Keep hope alive.”*

The other options are not correct in this context:

(A) “Hope and change” was Obama's slogan but not exactly a refrain from Jackson’s 1988 speech.  
(B) “Rainbow coalition” was used by Jackson but not echoed as a campaign slogan by Obama in 2008.  
(C) “Yankee go home” is unrelated.  
(D) “I have a dream” belongs to Martin Luther King Jr., not Jackson.
